ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 34731007 refers to coffee as a substance; no espresso-specific SNOMED CT code identified. Closest parent match used as the most defensible available anchor.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single espresso shot is approximately 30 ml (30 g), served in a small demitasse cup.

nutrient_source.title: Espresso, single shot — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for espresso single shot (cr_id G00010623). All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record. Saturated fat adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14209 as HPB does not separately report saturated fat fractions.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; plain black coffee and espresso
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain black coffee and espresso contain negligible digestible carbohydrates (1.67 g/100 g, of which 0 g is sugar). The University of Sydney GI Database classifies plain black coffee as having a GI of approximately 0 due to the absence of digestible starch or sugar. A single espresso shot (30 g) contributes approximately 0.5 g carbohydrate, yielding a glycemic load of 0.0 per serving. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (14 mg/100 g); no added sugar or cream in plain preparation
hypertension.guidance: A plain single espresso contains only 14 mg sodium per 100 g and zero added sugar, making it acceptable for hypertensive patients when consumed without added sugar, cream, or flavoured syrups, and when total daily caffeine is kept below 300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero sugar and negligible digestible carbohydrate (1.67 g/100 g); chlorogenic acids may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain black espresso contains zero sugar and only 1.67 g carbohydrate per 100 g, producing no meaningful glycaemic response; chlorogenic acids in coffee have been associated with improved insulin sensitivity, and consumption of 2–3 shots per day without added sugar or sweetened creamers is appropriate for individuals with type 2 diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low fat (0.18 g/100 g), zero cholesterol, low sodium; moderate caffeine intake is not contraindicated in most CVD patients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain espresso is very low in fat (0.18 g/100 g), contains zero cholesterol, and has negligible sodium (14 mg/100 g); moderate consumption of 1–2 shots per day is not contraindicated for most cardiovascular disease patients, though individuals with arrhythmias or uncontrolled hypertension should consult their physician regarding caffeine intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Chlorogenic acids and polyphenols with anti-inflammatory properties; zero sugar and zero saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Plain black espresso contains chlorogenic acids and polyphenols with anti-inflammatory properties; it is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis when consumed without added sugar or cream, limited to 2–3 shots per day to avoid sleep disruption that may worsen RA symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Espresso Single (also known as Espresso Sincle in Singapore HPB records, or Espresso Tunggal in Malay) is a single shot of espresso — a concentrated coffee beverage prepared by forcing hot pressurised water through finely ground, darkly roasted coffee beans. A standard single shot is approximately 30 ml and is served in a small demitasse cup. The resulting beverage is intensely flavoured, slightly bitter, with a characteristic reddish-brown crema on the surface. Per 100 g, it provides only 9 kcal, 0.12 g protein, 0.18 g fat, and 1.67 g carbohydrate with zero sugar, making it one of the lowest-calorie beverages available. Sodium is very low at 14 mg/100 g. Notably, magnesium content is relatively high at 80 mg/100 g and potassium at 115 mg/100 g, reflecting the mineral content of coffee beans. Espresso contains caffeine (approximately 60 mg per single shot) and chlorogenic acids, which are polyphenolic antioxidants associated with anti-inflammatory effects and potential improvement of insulin sensitivity. Clinically, plain black espresso is suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis when consumed without added sugar, cream, or flavoured syrups. In Singapore, espresso is widely consumed across all ethnic communities and is available in cafes, kopitiams, and food courts. The HPB Singapore Food Composition Database lists this item under Beverages > Coffee.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a single espresso?
Is espresso good for high blood pressure?
What is the sugar content of espresso?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database canonical record (cr_id G00010623) with confidence 0.95. The food is a well-defined, standardised beverage with consistent composition. All core macros and key micronutrients are present from the primary approved source.
notes: Sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Food Composition Database — plain espresso contains no added sugar and negligible naturally occurring sugars below detection threshold (confirmed zero). Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB — espresso is a liquid extract with no fibre. Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB — espresso contains no cholesterol. Saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.04 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central entry for brewed espresso (USDA SR Legacy, NDB 14209), as HPB does not separately report saturated fat; this is a minor adaptation. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for this item, and were not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14209 at the level of individual fatty acid fractions for this beverage. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg set to -1: full vitamin panel data are not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for this espresso entry; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14209 was consulted as a secondary reference but does not provide complete vitamin data for brewed espresso at this level of detail. Phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for this item, and were not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14209 for this specific beverage entry. Water_g set to -1: moisture content is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for this espresso entry. The input record uses the spelling 'Sincle' which is a Singaporean romanisation variant of 'Single'; both forms are preserved in aliases. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates.
